ORDER RE EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO STIPULATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. DEFENDANTS SHIFTS THE BURDEN BY PRODUCING EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT MAKE A PRIMA FACIE CASE OF PREMISES LIABILITY UNDER GOV. CODE 835. (SEE EVIDENCE CITED IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT OF UNDISPUTED MATERIAL FACTS ("UMF") 6 - 9, EXHIBITS B, C.) FURTHER, DEFENDANT HAS MADE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING OF DESIGN IMMUNITY UNDER GOV. CODE 830.6. THE DECLARATION OF MR. SALLABERRY SHOWS THAT THE STREET DESIGN FOR THE AREA WHERE THE ACCIDENT ALLEGEDLY OCCURRED WAS APPROVED BY EMPLOYEES OF THE SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY AND THAT THE DESIGN WAS REASONABLE BECAUSE IT CONFORMED TO ACCEPTED STANDARDS. (SEE SALLABERRY DECL., 4 - 9, EXHIBIT A.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.
TRIAL COMMENCED.
JURY TRIAL SET FOR AUG-25-2014 CONTINUED TO AUG-26-2014 AT 9:00 AM IN 504 FOR JURY SELECTION. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION And Ntc
Off Calendar Jun-13-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. No opposition filed. Defendants shifts the burden by producing evidence that Plaintiff cannot make a prima facie case of premises liability under Gov. Code 835. (See Evidence Cited in Support of Defendant's Separate Statement of Undisputed Material Facts ("UMF") 6 - 9, Exhibits B, C.) Further, Defendant has made a prima facie showing of design immunity under Gov. Code 830.6. The declaration of Mr. Sallaberry shows that the street design for the area where the accident allegedly occurred was approved by employees of the San Francisco Municipal Transportation Agency and that the design was reasonable because it conformed to accepted standards. (See Sallaberry Decl., 4 - 9, Exhibit A.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Continued To Aug-25-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
